items.prompt: Find the area of the segment AYB of a circle of radius 21 cm in which ∠AOB = 120°, given that the corresponding sector OAYB has area 462 cm² and triangle OAB has area 441/4 √3 cm². Give the segment area in cm². (Use √3 = 1.732)
items.quote: Therefore, area of the segment AYB = ( 462 – 441/4 √3 ) cm²     [From (1), (2) and (3)]

items.prompt: Find the area of the sector of a circle with radius 4 cm and of angle 30°. (Use π = 3.14)
Area of a sector of angle p (in degrees) of a circle with radius R is: (A) p/180 × 2πR  (B) p/180 × πR²  (C) p/360 × 2πR  (D) p/720 × 2πR²
Using the formula for the length of an arc of a sector, find the length (in cm) of the arc of a sector of angle 120° in a circle of radius 21 cm. (Use π = 22/7)
items.quote: So, length of an arc of a sector of angle θ = θ/360 × 2πr

items.prompt: For a circle with radius 4 cm and π = 3.14, the minor sector of angle 30° has area 4.19 cm². Find the area of the corresponding major sector in cm².
In triangle OAB, the base AB = 21√3 cm and the height OM = 21/2 cm. Find the area of triangle OAB in cm² (use √3 = 1.732).
In a circle of radius 21 cm with ∠AOB = 120°, OM is drawn perpendicular to chord AB, meeting it at M. What is the measure of ∠AOM in degrees?
items.quote: So, M is the mid-point of AB and ∠ AOM = ∠ BOM = 1/2 × 120° = 60° .

items.prompt: In triangle OAB with OA = OB and OM ⊥ AB, which congruence criterion proves ΔAMO ≅ ΔBMO? (A) SSS (B) ASA (C) RHS (D) AAA
items.quote: Therefore, by RHS congruence, Δ AMO ≅ Δ BMO.

items.prompt: In right triangle OMA with hypotenuse OA = 21 cm and ∠AOM = 60°, OM/OA = cos 60°. Find OM in cm.
In right triangle OMA with hypotenuse OA = 21 cm and ∠AOM = 60°, AM/OA = sin 60° = √3/2. Find AM in cm (use √3 = 1.732).
The perpendicular from the centre bisects chord AB at M, and AM = 21√3/2 cm. Find the full chord length AB in cm (use √3 = 1.732).
The minor sector area is 462 cm² and the triangle OAB area is 441√3/4 cm² (≈190.95 cm²). Find the area of the segment AYB in cm².
items.quote: Therefore, area of the segment AYB = ( 462 – 441/4 √3 ) cm²     [From (1), (2) and (3)]

items.prompt: Substituting π = 22/7, evaluate the area of the sector OAYB of a circle of radius 21 cm with central angle 120°: area = 120/360 × 22/7 × 21 × 21 cm². Give the area in cm².
items.quote: Now, area of the sector OAYB = 120/360 × 22/7 × 21 × 21 cm² = 462 cm²     (2)

items.prompt: Using the alternative method, the major sector of a circle (radius 4 cm, minor angle 30°, π = 3.14) has angle (360 − 30)°. Compute its area in cm².
